summary: Full backend + frontend testing of Danish Tidsregistrering app. Backend: 11/11 pytest tests passed covering users (25 seeded), projects (4), activities (16, mixed levels/statuses), time-entry upsert (verified idempotency), save-week, copy-previous (date shifting +7 days verified), my-activities add/remove, favorite toggle, activity CRUD (project + program-level), and /overview aggregation. Frontend: All 4 screens render, header navigation works, user-switcher shows 25 users. Indtast timer: week grid editable, decimal comma '2,5' accepted, Tab moves focus to next column (verified via document.activeElement), debounced auto-save works, row/day/week totals update reactively, 'Gem uge' shows 'Gemt for ugen', prev/this-week navigation works, search popover adds new activity row, 'Kopiér sidste uge' fires and toast 'Kopierede 3 aktiviteter fra sidste uge' shown. Mine aktiviteter: filter chips, favoriter toggle, add/remove activity, toast feedback. Programoverblik: 5 breakdown rows (4 projects + Programniveau), KPI tiles (Timer i alt, Uger gemt %, Manglende), missing-users list. Administration: new activity dialog, status flip (Åbn), edit, delete (with confirm) all working.

minor.issue: When the destination week already has saved=true entries for an activity, copy-previous merges entries dict and sets saved=False, silently invalidating an already-saved week. Consider only resetting saved status for newly-created rows, or document the behavior.

action_items: (Optional) Reconsider copy-previous side-effect of resetting saved=true to saved=false on pre-existing destination entries.
(Optional cosmetic) Mine aktiviteter filter chip data-testid keys are English ('all','project','program') while labels are Danish – consistent but worth noting for spec-bound automation.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is single-file (~613 lines) – acceptable for MVP, but consider splitting routers (users/projects/activities/time/overview/seed) into modules as the app grows.
auto_seed_if_empty uses on_event('startup') which is deprecated in modern FastAPI; switch to lifespan handler when convenient.
Pydantic v2 ConfigDict(extra='ignore') is correctly used; _id is excluded via projection {'_id': 0} everywhere - good.
POST /api/time-entries/save-week and /copy-previous accept user_id and week_start_date as query params (not body); the frontend wrapper passes them via params – consistent and OK.
Frontend uses process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL correctly; no hardcoded URLs.
test_credentials: No authentication required. App auto-selects first medarbejder user; switch via data-testid='user-select-trigger'.
seed_data_creation: Auto-seeded on startup when DB empty (25 users, 4 projects, 16 activities, time entries for current+previous week for first 20 users). Tests created/cleaned TEST_-prefixed activities and ephemeral time-entries in future weeks (Monday+14 / +21 / +28 days) to avoid touching seed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_tidsregistrering.py and are idempotent; run via 'pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v'. They use future-dated weeks to avoid clobbering seed sample entries and clean up after themselves. Frontend has comprehensive data-testid coverage (nav-*, user-select-trigger, user-option-*, indtast-timer-page, week-title, btn-prev-week/btn-this-week/btn-next-week/btn-copy-previous/btn-save-week, btn-search-activity, activity-search-input, activity-result-*, row-*/cell-*-*/row-total-*/day-total-*/week-total, status-badge, mine-aktiviteter-page, filter-all|project|program, btn-toggle-fav, activity-row-*, fav-*, remove-*, add-*, programoverblik-page, ovw-breakdown, ovw-row-*, missing-*, admin-page, btn-new-activity, activity-dialog, form-name|level|project|owner|status|requires-note|submit|cancel, admin-search, admin-filter-*, admin-section-*, admin-row-*, open-*, close-*, edit-*, delete-*). User context persisted in localStorage as 'tr_current_user_id'.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. One behavioral note: copy-previous resets saved status of pre-existing destination entries to false (intentional but could be unexpected if a user re-runs copy after saving).
